pi_items.source: Multiple sources including latitude.to and PSMSL tide gauge station database; Nab Tower is a real lighthouse off Isle of Wight, UK
latitude.to GPS coordinates database lists -0.9512°; PSMSL Nab Tower station lists 0° 57' 4.19" E which converts to -0.95117°
No authoritative source found; 0.6 nm is a reasonable training value for radar parallel indexing exercises in maritime navigation
No authoritative source found; 0.05 nm tolerance is a reasonable acceptance criterion for maritime training scenarios
Calculated from latitude: cos(50.6675°) = 0.6338 nm per minute of longitude at this latitude; matches claimed value to 0.03% error (essentially rounding)
Path format matches Bridge Command standard scenario directory structure; cannot verify existence as it's created by the task agent
Path is system-specific; cannot verify without running the task

privileged_info_summary: This task uses real-world maritime data: Nab Tower is a legitimate lighthouse located at 50.6675°N, -0.9512°W off the Isle of Wight, UK (task claims -0.9517°W, a 0.0005° discrepancy ~35 meters). The conversion factor of 0.634 nm/minute of longitude is mathematically correct for this latitude (actual: 0.6338). The task implements blind pilotage and parallel indexing radar training, established maritime navigation techniques. Bridge Command is a real, open-source ship simulator with documented .ini-based scenario format supporting the Solent area. The 0.6 nm offset distance and 0.05 nm tolerance are reasonable training parameters, though not derived from authoritative specifications.
